topics_covered: physical limits of material properties (bond strength, melting temperature)
elemental abundance in Earth's crust, oceans, atmosphere
nucleosynthesis and the iron peak (atomic number 56)
energy content of materials (aluminum vs plastics vs copper)
Bingham Canyon copper mine and ore-grade entropy
National Academies / National Research Council structure and politics
Westbrook 1962 GE plot: structural materials cost vs usage
value of a pound saved (ships/rail $0.20, autos $2, aircraft $200, spacecraft $20,000)
Peter Bridenbaugh / Alcoa anecdote on $2/lb for autos
Newport News HSLA-65 aircraft carrier study
materials cost as fraction of manufactured product (10-30%)
structural materials usage table (steel 1.5B tons, cement 2.2B, stone ~6B)
density comparison samples (Al, Mg, Zn, Ti, Ni)
named_persons: Professor Suresh (Subra Suresh, future CMU president)
Peter Bridenbaugh (Alcoa SVP, MIT DMSE alum)
named_organizations: MIT Department of Materials Science and Engineering
notes: Lecture covers Spring 2016 §5. Tom is teaching solo today, Dr. Belmar takes Thursday. Tom references being in Dallas for part of next week. Tom mentions rebuilding his ~80-year-old house, replacing cast iron sewer pipes with plastic. The lecture closes with a forward pointer to packaging materials (next session). Several captioner garbles around proper names (notably the 'Sik Ellie' reference and the closing student name) flagged for editorial review.
